question_text: Two transparent materials have different refractive indices: Material A has a refractive index of 1.5 and Material B has a refractive index of 2.4. A light ray hits the boundary between these two materials at an angle of incidence of 35° (measured from the normal). Which statement correctly compares the behaviour of light at this boundary?

options.label: A
options.text: The light will refract at the same angle in both materials because the angle of incidence is the same

options.label: B
options.text: If light enters Material B from Material A, it will bend towards the normal and the angle of refraction will be less than 35°

options.label: C
options.text: If light enters Material B from Material A, it will bend away from the normal because Material B is denser

options.label: D
options.text: The angle of refraction will always be exactly half the angle of incidence regardless of which material the light enters

correct_answer: B) If light enters Material B from Material A, it will bend towards the normal and the angle of refraction will be less than 35°
explanation: Material B has a higher refractive index than Material A, meaning it is optically denser. When light enters a denser material, it always bends towards the normal, so the refracted angle will be smaller than the incident angle of 35°.
